note: Synthetic append-only log for the access-provisioning process from the intake example. Baseline cycle time ~6 business days; target 2. Shows performance improving toward target AND adoption/reuse growing over three monthly readings.

entries.note: First reading after pilot launch (predefined role bundles for one application).

adoption_reuse.reusability_rationale: Works for the pilot application's standard roles; privileged/admin access still handled manually.

entries.note: Rework rate also dropping - role bundles prevent most wrong-role requests before they're submitted.

adoption_reuse.reusability_rationale: A second application adopted the same request-approve-provision pattern with only role-bundle changes; privileged access still open.

entries.note: Approaching target. The redundant data-governance sign-off was removed after confirming it duplicated the security segregation-of-duties review (no control lost), taking ~1 day out of the chain.

adoption_reuse.reusability_rationale: Templated the request -> automated SoD check -> provision -> recertify pattern; two other applications adopted it. Candidate to scale enterprise-wide (see process-improve).

readout: cycle_time: 2.3 business_days as of 2026-06-30 - DOWN 62% vs baseline (6 -> 2.3), 93% of the way to the 2-day target. adoption_rate: 91% (rising -> sustained), reuse_count: 2 other applications. Reusability now 'yes' -> flag for enterprise scale.
